title: Post-expansion liquidity reset
plain_language_summary_zh: 研究短时扩张后回归是否只在成交与波动同时降温时出现。
falsifiable_hypothesis: After an hourly range expansion, bars with simultaneous volume and realized-volatility normalization revert more often than expansion bars without normalization.
proposed_market_mechanism: Temporary liquidity imbalance may dissipate when participation and volatility normalize together.
supporting_evidence: Approved Development datasets include the 1h and 4h series required for a descriptive comparison.
contradictory_evidence: Normalization can also mark a pause before continuation.

source_refs.claim: The frozen state binds the approved Development datasets and timeframes.

novelty_vs_existing_research: Separates cooling participation from price distance instead of assuming every expansion mean-reverts.
minimal_test_method: Measure pre-declared three-bar reversion labels after expansion, stratified by joint volume and volatility normalization.
comparison_baseline: All hourly range-expansion observations without the joint normalization condition.
falsification_conditions: The conditioned group does not improve reversion incidence consistently across approved pairs.
stop_conditions: Stop if either normalization input is unavailable from the sealed Development artifacts.
known_limitations: A descriptive label does not represent executable fill quality.
